steps.title: Preserve the main verb
steps.content: The main subject is American abstract artist Richard Serra, and the main verb later in the sentence is assembles. The words in the blank must identify Serra and add a nonfinite phrase about his intention, not create another main verb before assembles.

steps.title: Avoid competing finite verbs
steps.content: Choice A creates the finite verb is intending before the sentence reaches assembles. Choice B creates the finite verb intends and interrupts the subject awkwardly. Choice D also uses intends as a finite verb, leaving assembles improperly attached. The answer is C.
